# semantic-recall

> Persistent semantic memory for LLM apps, AI agents, and chatbots — zero config, zero API keys, works fully offline.

## Package

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/semantic-recall
- GitHub: https://github.com/skt329/semantic-recall
- License: MIT
- Node.js: >= 18

## What It Does

`semantic-recall` gives any LLM app, AI agent, or chatbot persistent memory using local embeddings and SQLite.
No vector database. No API keys. No Docker. Just `npm install semantic-recall` and two methods: `remember()` and `recall()`.

Memories are stored as semantic embeddings using Transformers.js (runs fully offline after the first ~25 MB model download).
Retrieval uses cosine similarity search, so `recall("dinner preferences")` correctly surfaces `"user is vegetarian"`.

## Core API

```typescript
import { Memory } from 'semantic-recall'

const memory = new Memory({ userId: 'user_123' })

// Store a fact — fire-and-forget, never throws
memory.remember("User prefers dark mode")

// Retrieve semantically relevant facts for your LLM prompt
const facts = await memory.recall("What UI settings does this user prefer?")
// → ["User prefers dark mode"]
```

## Key Features

- Zero infrastructure: SQLite on disk, embeddings run in a worker thread
- Works offline: local Transformers.js model (Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
- Crash-safe: persistent queue with exponential backoff and dead letter queue
- Auto deduplication: configurable cosine similarity threshold
- TTL / auto-expiry: supports `"7d"`, `"1h"`, `"30m"` formats + global `defaultTtl`
- Tags: categorize memories and filter on recall (AND logic)
- Batch operations: `rememberMany()` with partial-failure resilience
- Update in-place: `update()` re-embeds automatically
- Related memories: semantic neighbor discovery via `related()`
- Stats & observability: `stats()`, `listNamespaces()`, event emitter
- Export / Import: portable JSON for backups and migrations
- Multi-tenant: userId + namespace scoping
- LLM auto-extraction: extract facts from raw conversation messages
- Storage adapters: SQLite (default), Turso, Supabase, or custom via BaseStorageAdapter
- Embedder adapters: local (default), OpenAI, or custom function
- Full TypeScript support with exported types

## Use Cases

- Add persistent memory to OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, or any LLM chatbot
- Give AI agents memory across sessions in agentic frameworks
- Use inside Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or any AI coding assistant context
- Mem0 alternative for developers who want zero infrastructure
- LangChain memory alternative that works without the full framework
- Persistent user context for Vercel AI SDK apps
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) compatible memory layer

## Installation

```bash
npm install semantic-recall
```

## Full Documentation

See: https://github.com/skt329/semantic-recall/blob/main/README.md
